answer.text: Buying a call at a lower strike and selling a call at a higher strike (same expiry)
Buying calls at two different strikes
Selling calls at two different strikes
Buying a call and selling a put at the same strike

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Bull call spread: Long call (lower strike) + Short call (higher strike). The long call profits from price rise; the short call caps the profit at the higher strike but reduces the net premium cost. **The Trap:** Selling a put with a call is a different strategy (risk reversal/combo), not a spread. **Cross-Exam Context:** Option spreads are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Max profit = Higher strike - Lower strike - Net premium. Max loss = Net premium paid. It's a limited profit, limited loss strategy.
